<reason>I need to identify the specific scientist from the 1961 documentary "The Thinking Machine" who gave the most optimistic (soonest) prediction for the arrival of thinking machines. Based on the footage and historical context of that specific CBS "Tomorrow" special, Marvin Minsky is the one known for the most aggressive timelines, often cited as predicting AI within a generation or "sooner than most people expect."</reason>

The 1961 documentary *The Thinking Machine*, part of the CBS "Tomorrow" series, features prominent AI pioneers including Claude Shannon, Jerome Wiesner, and Marvin Minsky. In the film, while Shannon and Wiesner discuss the technical hurdles and the nature of intelligence, Marvin Minsky provides the most optimistic outlook on the timeline for creating "thinking machines." Minsky famously predicted that the creation of artificial intelligence was a problem that could be solved within a generation, stating it would happen "sooner" than many anticipated.

<final_answer>Marvin Minsky</final_answer>
